_notes: Local registry of story arcs the cogni-research writer can apply as section structure. Mirrors a subset of cogni-narrative's arc-definition.md files. Audited by the audit-arcs skill against the upstream source of truth. v1 ships two arcs: standard-research (today's default, dynamic structure) and corporate-visions (executive narrative arc, EN+DE, detailed report_type only). The compatible_densities field is research-local (no cogni-narrative counterpart) — missing field defaults to ["standard"] in the validator.

standard-research.notes: Default structure. Sections are generated dynamically from sub-questions per the report-type template baked into the writer agent (Introduction → topical sections per sub-question → optional Cross-Cutting Analysis → Conclusion → References). No fixed elements or proportions. The writer falls through to its existing per-report-type template logic when this arc is selected. The reviewer's Arc-Structural Gate is skipped. This arc is functionally equivalent to leaving story_arc_id unset — it's surfaced as an explicit menu option so the configuration model is uniform (every project has an arc, even if it's the default one). compatible_densities lists the prose_density values this arc supports — the dynamic structure compresses cleanly under executive density, so both standard (today's default) and executive (Pyramid Principle + BLUF, target_words as ceiling) are valid.

elements.purpose: Open with the most surprising finding from the research — a counterintuitive data point or paradigm-shifting insight. Lives inside the Why Change section, not as a separate H2.

Reframe research findings as an unconsidered need — a problem decision-makers didn't know they had, or didn't realize was solvable. Use Problem-Solution-Benefit (PSB) structure with contrast framing ("Most organizations think X. But research shows Y.") and end with competitive implication.

elements.heading_de: Warum jetzt: Das sich schließende Zeitfenster
elements.purpose: Establish urgency through forcing functions — external pressures, deadlines, tipping points. Stack 2-3 forcing functions with specific timelines and quantified consequences. Use before/after contrasts (early movers vs. late starters).

Convert strategic recommendations into Power Positions — capabilities that create competitive advantage and are difficult to replicate. Use IS-DOES-MEANS structure for each position with You-Phrasing throughout the DOES layer and a competitive moat in MEANS.

Quantify the cost of inaction through compound impact calculation — stack 3-4 cost dimensions (regulatory penalties, talent premium, market position loss, opportunity cost) over a 3-year horizon. End with a simple ratio: "Action costs less than inaction by 2-3x."

transitions.hook_to_why_change: This gap between [observation] and [reality] defines the challenge.
transitions.why_change_to_why_now: Three converging forces make action urgent.
transitions.why_now_to_why_you: Organizations that thrive don't just react — they build capabilities.

corporate-visions.closing_pattern: Simple, undeniable business case comparison (e.g., "Action costs less than inaction by 2-3x.")
